materialChanges: Replaced the unsupported DLMF record with an official standards source whose exact High School Functions locators cover function notation, graph features, composition, average rate, and transformations; remapped every affected claim and scene source note.
Added a bounded average-rate claim with conditions, units, interval interpretation, uncertainty, and complete scene/source-note coverage.

Aligned the mastery gate by mapping the quadratic range task to the function-interpretation objective and replacing the table item with a text-equivalent scaled-axis, interval, intercept, and rate task.
Qualified the transformation rule for positive and negative translation parameters and preserved the independently recalculated answer keys.

Converted diagnostic, misconception, practice, transfer, and synthesis prompts to semantic checks with attempt-before-feedback order; added prerequisite-only routing, misconception prediction and recheck, retrieval feedback, and transfer units.
Removed descriptive prose from acceptable-response arrays and updated attribution to identify the revised evidence basis and structured review/finalization provenance.

reviewDispositions.rationale: Added claim-average-rate with distinct-endpoint conditions, output-change/input-change definition, units, interval scope, and an instantaneous-rate boundary; mapped it to all six scenes that teach or apply the idea and updated every affected source note.

Removed the mismatched DLMF record and replaced it with source-common-core-functions, an official standards record with exact HSF-IF.A.1–2, HSF-IF.B.4–6, HSF-BF.A.1c, and HSF-BF.B.3 locators; claims and all learner-visible notes now cite only sources attached to their support.

Mapped item-transfer-quadratic, which explicitly elicits range, to objective-interpret-function. Replaced item-table-interpretation with a non-unit two-axis-scale task that requires coordinate conversion, a y-intercept, decreasing intervals, and a labeled interval rate, with matching answer logic and rubric criteria.
Rewrote the f(x-h)+k rule to state right/up behavior for positive parameters and left/down behavior by absolute magnitude for negative parameters.

The confirmed quadratic transformations, maximum, range, intercepts, average rate, and verification table remain mathematically correct; independent recalculation also found the other keyed results correct.
Wrapped the three starting questions in a semantic check and added concrete answer reasoning plus an actionable algebra/coordinate recovery route after the attempt.
Removed the pre-instruction transformation question and replaced it with solving u-4=0, a prerequisite algebra check with feedback and recovery guidance.
Added an initial prediction and key-input commitment before the misconception explanation, followed by a new p(x-5) recheck and reasoned feedback.

Converted all four practice prompts to semantic check directives, added an explicit attempt-before-continuing instruction, and retained feedback after each closed prompt so the source remains complete in reading and print paths.

Placed the independent transfer prompt in a semantic check and kept its complete solution under the existing post-attempt feedback heading, preserving both retrieval order and noninteractive export parity.
Wrapped the synthesis retrieval questions in a semantic check and added concise post-attempt answers that explain function identity, domain/range, horizontal shifts, and average-rate reasoning.
Removed the descriptive grading criterion from item-average-rate acceptableResponses; the array is now empty and interpretation remains in the answer logic and rubric.
Removed the descriptive table criterion from item-transfer-quadratic acceptableResponses; the array is now empty and the required evidence remains in the rubric.

Relabeled the worked-example answer as feedback after attempting while retaining the documented format-v1 source order: the semantic check closes before feedback, with no unsupported hiding convention added.
Defined the transfer input as distance in centimetres and output as signal in volts, then interpreted the computed rate as -0.5 volts per centimetre.
The note confirms the candidate identity, scene order, objectives, file mappings, and varied square-root transfer design; lesson.json therefore remains unchanged at version 0.1.0 and draft status.

evidenceChecked: All seven scenes retain one level-one heading, logical source order, balanced documented directives, and text-first prompts followed by feedback; no task depends on hover, drag, timing, color, sound, or position.

Equations, graph relationships, transformations, axis scales, coordinates, intervals, and rates are expressed in selectable Markdown text, LaTeX, lists, or tables with units and prose interpretation suitable for nonvisual and print routes.
The pack contains no figures, audio, video, datasets, third-party assets, personal data, patient material, remote embeds, executable code, raw HTML, JavaScript, or HTML entities.

Every mapped claim appears in its scene source note with all supporting source IDs; reference records contain allowed types, bounded fields, single ISO dates, facts-only use, rights evidence, and dated agent-access observations.

The Common Core and NIST material is used only as factual or standards evidence; lesson wording, derivations, functions, tables, examples, feedback, and assessments are independently created, and ATTRIBUTION.md records that boundary.

Author, reviewer, and finalizer responsibility is disclosed through lesson metadata, review artifacts, attribution, and this adjudication; the lesson remains educational, non-clinical, non-credentialing, and free of privacy-sensitive collection.
limitations: No browser rendering, screen-reader session, keyboard traversal, 200% zoom/reflow test, reduced-motion test, or non-web export was run; this audit is based on the final semantic Markdown and structured pack.

The operator expressly prohibited npm and repository scripts, so full schema validation, strict source preflight, renderer checks, and repository verification were not run. JSON parsing, internal linkage, claim-note coverage, field limits, answer types, rubric totals, directive balance, and raw-markup checks passed targeted local inspection.

The scope prohibited the site policy and review schema and provided no live GitHub transport access. Review-input eligibility therefore relies on the supplied non-dismissed COMMENTED status plus the locally checked identities, candidate SHA, roles, verdicts, conflicts, signed times, principals, providers, and unique run IDs.
A candidate OpenStax source was excluded after its current page disclosed an AI-use restriction; the final pack neither cites nor relies on that source.
The final content commit does not yet exist, so finalCommit remains the operator-required placeholder pending the authorized commit and runtime stamping.

rationale: The standard input gate is satisfied by exactly two advisory artifacts against candidate 6a6ce2dc1fc88c58b36b789d3c433c633ac867c0: one academic and one learning-design review, both request-changes, across two distinct declared runs and two declared model families/providers. The two artifacts and operator-supplied COMMENTED transport status are counted; no review is excluded. Their 16 indexed findings are all disposed: 14 are incorporated and two correctness/structure notes require no change. The single coherent revision replaces the unsupported evidence record, adds complete average-rate claim coverage, closes range/scale/interval assessment gaps, corrects transformation sign conditions, strengthens prerequisite routing and misconception repair, enforces semantic attempt-before-feedback order, supplies retrieval feedback and transfer units, and removes invalid acceptable-response prose. Independent recalculation confirms the numerical keys, while targeted local checks pass JSON parsing, identity/version/status preservation, minute totals, reference field constraints, claim/source/scene-note integrity, objective/item/rubric linkage, string-only acceptable responses, Markdown structure, and inert-content boundaries. The final accessibility-and-rights audit finds equivalent text routes, original expression, facts-only source use, complete attribution/provenance, no third-party assets or personal data, and no executable or remote content. Both original reviews requested changes and did not review this final edit; under standard-lesson-v3 the fresh finalizer owns that bounded revision and audit rather than starting another cohort. The decision is merge subject to exact final-commit substitution, runtime identity stamping, required external validation, and the maintainer-only publication transition. Declared run/provider diversity is attested provenance, not verified independence.
dissent: Both eligible advisory reviews returned request-changes on the original candidate; neither reviewer approved or inspected the finalizer's post-review revision.

Principal-level independence is absent because github:VSBDev is the accountable operator for authoring, reviews, and finalization; the learning-design review declared this conflict, and founding-stage policy treats the distinct agent runs/providers as disclosed and attested rather than independently verified.
No real learner testing occurred, and no rendered assistive-technology or export evaluation was available in this restricted run.
conditions: The operator must commit the final lesson content, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with that exact 40-character SHA, and runtime-stamp all four RUNTIME-STAMPED adjudicator identity fields before submitting the adjudication; teaching content must not change after that commit.

Before merge, the operator must run the repository-required strict source preflight and validation outside this restricted run and resolve any failure without misrepresenting this adjudication as already validated.
The maintainer must leave version 0.1.0 unchanged and make only the separately authorized publication transition for lesson status, source confidence, and retained claim review-status fields.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev — accountable operator, fresh finalizer and adjudicator
